purpose: Detect likely MIME type and file category from file extension and optional leading bytes for upload, preview, and routing workflows.

endpoints.description: Detect likely MIME type from filename and optional base64 encoded header bytes.

dependencies.purpose: Detect binary file signatures from leading bytes
Resolve MIME types from filename extensions

sidecar_specification.purpose: MIME type detection for local files and upload descriptors.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a workflow needs to classify a file before preview, validation, storage, or routing.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a malware scanner, content parser, or security proof that a file is safe.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Provide filename and optional first bytes as base64. 2) Call /mime/detect. 3) Route by mime_type or category.

failure_modes.condition: Unknown extension and no recognizable signature
failure_modes.recovery: Provide header bytes or handle file as generic binary

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use file-type to inspect Buffer.from(header_base64, 'base64') when present, then fall back to mime-types lookup(filename). Return application/octet-stream when neither source matches.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Extensions can lie and very short headers may not be enough for signature detection. Never mark confidence as 1.0 for extension-only detection.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Prefer signature results over extension results and include source so callers can decide how much to trust it.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not read arbitrary file paths, do not execute file content, and do not call external MIME databases.
